summary: The page shell reserves a container slot for the input UI; the input UI renders its textarea into that slot. The shell also provides a visible label that the textarea is associated with.

slot.ownership: Shell owns the container element and surrounding layout/labels; input UI owns the textarea and any input-internal markup placed inside the container.
slot.label_contract: Shell provides a visible <label> (or heading) text identifying the input region. Input UI's textarea must be associated with that label via for/id pairing OR the input UI may carry its own aria-label. Either satisfies S2.A3.

stable_attributes.input_textarea: The textarea exposes data-role='latex-input' (or a documented id) so other sections can locate it without coupling to layout.

data_passed: Output (shell -> input UI): a container DOM element reference where the textarea should be appended, OR a stable container selector.
No runtime data flows through this contract — it is a structural/DOM contract only.
non_goals: Shell does not implement input event handling.
Input UI does not implement page-level layout outside its container.
